# UC-13.7.11 · Fluentd Tag Routing Misconfiguration — Unmatched Records and Null Route

## Description

After adding new container namespaces or syslog facilities, tags often fail to match `<match>` stanzas, silently sending events to the wrong store or dropping them. Log signatures for unmatched tags expose config gaps quickly.

## Value

Stops blind spots when Kubernetes labels change or legacy apps rotate facility codes.

## Implementation

Augment with a metrics counter from `fluent-plugin-prometheus` for `router_no_match`. Map `config_file` to Git repo paths. Alert on new `tag` values appearing in this search.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "pattern not matched" OR "no patterns matched" OR "record is suppressed" OR "disable_router" OR "unmatched"
| rex field=_raw "tag=(?<tag>[\\w._-]+)"
| stats count, values(tag) as tags by host, config_file
| where count >= 20
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Treemap (host × tag), table of top unmatched tags, diff vs previous week.

## References

- [Fluentd — Config syntax](https://docs.fluentd.org/configuration/config-file)
